issue.title: pnpm lint has 36 pre-existing errors and isn't in CI — clear them + gate lint

successCriteria.description: pnpm -C apps/desktop lint exits 0 (zero errors): src-tauri/target+gen ignored (kills 3 false parse errors), no-unused-vars ignores _-prefixed discards (kills 9), the 1 no-useless-escape + 1 stale eslint-disable fixed

The react-hooks v7 React-Compiler advisory rules (set-state-in-effect, refs, purity, preserve-manual-memoization) + exhaustive-deps + react-refresh/only-export-components are a DELIBERATE warn-level config (not blanket-disabled, not error) with a documented rationale; they remain visible as warnings
Lint runs in CI (pr.yml) gating on errors=0; a guard test asserts the lint step is present so it can't silently be removed
pnpm build + full pnpm test + cargo green; the #279 fresh-ref no-restricted-syntax rule + freshRefGuard stay green; lint errors=0 confirmed

phases.title: Deliberate eslint config + targeted fixes -> lint errors=0
phases.content:
Edit `apps/desktop/eslint.config.js` and 2 source files so `pnpm -C apps/desktop lint` exits 0 (zero ERRORS; warnings are an accepted, deliberate signal).

1. eslint.config.js:
   a. globalIgnores: add `src-tauri/target` and `src-tauri/gen` to the existing `['dist']` ignore. (The 3 'Parsing error: Unexpected character' are eslint choking on tauri build artifacts under src-tauri/target/.../tauri-codegen-assets/*.js — they are NOT source; ignoring target/ kills all 3 and stops eslint wasting time on build output.)
   b. Configure `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` with `{ argsIgnorePattern: '^_', varsIgnorePattern: '^_', caughtErrorsIgnorePattern: '^_' }` (override the recommended preset's default). The 9 violations are all intentional `_`-prefixed destructuring discards (`_removed`, `_r1`, `_get`, `_removedTabs`...) — the `_` prefix is the standard 'intentionally unused' convention; this honors it. Keep no-unused-vars at ERROR for genuinely-unused (non-`_`) vars.
   c. DELIBERATE rule levels (add a clearly-commented rules block explaining WHY): downgrade to 'warn' the react-hooks v7 React-Compiler advisory rules — `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect`, `react-hooks/refs`, `react-hooks/purity`, `react-hooks/preserve-manual-memoization` — (the project is on React 19 but has NOT adopted the React Compiler; these are advisory and fire on deliberate existing patterns), AND `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` (conventionally warn — error-level is famously too aggressive), AND `react-refresh/only-export-components` (a dev-fast-refresh nicety, not correctness). KEEP at ERROR: `react-hooks/rules-of-hooks` (real correctness), the typescript-eslint recommended errors, AND the #279 fresh-ref `no-restricted-syntax` rule (do NOT downgrade it). Comment each downgrade with the reason + that they're tracked for incremental cleanup.
2. `apps/desktop/src/components/ui/IssueFormModal.tsx:30`: fix the `no-useless-escape` (`\[` -> `[` inside the regex/string — verify the regex still matches the same; it's an unnecessary escape of `[` outside a character class context, so removing the backslash is behavior-preserving).
3. `apps/desktop/src/components/ui/ErrorBoundary.tsx:37`: remove the stale `eslint-disable` directive (the warning says no problem was reported from 'no-console' there — the disable is dead).
4. Run `pnpm -C apps/desktop lint`: assert it exits 0 with ZERO errors (warnings remain — report the warning count). Run `pnpm build` (tsc -b) clean and `pnpm -C apps/desktop test` green (freshRefGuard + all). Confirm the #279 fresh-ref rule still fires (it's still error-level) — a quick planted-violation check is optional but reassuring.
Gates: lint errors=0; build clean; desktop vitest green.
verification: pnpm -C apps/desktop lint exits 0 (zero errors); warning count reported
src-tauri/target+gen ignored; no-unused-vars _-pattern; IssueFormModal escape + ErrorBoundary stale-disable fixed
advisory rules downgraded to warn with documented rationale; rules-of-hooks + #279 rule stay error
build clean; desktop vitest green; freshRefGuard green
phases.summary: eslint.config.js: globalIgnores += src-tauri/target + src-tauri/gen (kills 3 false parse errors on tauri codegen artifacts); @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars stays error with argsIgnorePattern/varsIgnorePattern/caughtErrorsIgnorePattern/destructuredArrayIgnorePattern '^_' (clears 9 intentional _-discards); commented warn-downgrade block for react-hooks v7 React-Compiler advisory rules (set-state-in-effect, refs, purity, preserve-manual-memoization) + exhaustive-deps + react-refresh/only-export-components (project hasn't adopted Compiler; tracked for incremental cleanup); rules-of-hooks stays error; #279 no-restricted-syntax fresh-ref rule untouched (still error — verified via planted-violation). IssueFormModal.tsx:30 removed needless \[ inside char class (behavior-identical). ErrorBoundary.tsx:37 removed dead eslint-disable. pnpm lint: exit 0, 0 errors, 31 warnings (all the downgraded rules). build clean; desktop vitest 267. testResults: { framework: "eslint+vitest", command: "pnpm -C apps/desktop lint + test", passed: 267, failed: 0, skipped: 0, status: "passed" }.

phases.title: Gate lint in CI (pr.yml) + guard test
phases.content:
Wire lint into CI so it can't rot again, and pin it.

1. `.github/workflows/pr.yml`: add a lint step in the existing job, after the `pnpm typecheck` step (line ~68) and before or after `pnpm build`. Step: `name: Lint` / `run: pnpm -C apps/desktop lint`. Default eslint exit is non-zero ONLY on errors (warnings don't fail), so this gates on errors=0 exactly as intended — do NOT add `--max-warnings 0` (the downgraded advisory rules are deliberate warnings and must not block). Keep the step on the existing ubuntu-22.04 job. (Windows-PR-CI is a separate Tier-2 item, out of scope.)
2. Guard test: add `packages/framework/__tests__/lint-ci-guard.test.mjs` (mirror `release-readiness-guard.test.mjs` style — read the workflow file, assert a substring). Assert `.github/workflows/pr.yml` contains a step running `pnpm -C apps/desktop lint` (so the lint gate can't be silently removed). Actionable failure message.
3. Run `pnpm -C packages/framework test` (new guard green) and confirm `pnpm -C apps/desktop lint` still exits 0 from phase 1.
Gates: framework suite green incl. the new guard; lint errors=0.
verification: pr.yml has a Lint step running pnpm -C apps/desktop lint (no --max-warnings, gates on errors)
lint-ci-guard.test.mjs asserts the step is present; green
framework suite green; lint still exits 0
phases.summary: pr.yml gained a Lint step (pnpm -C apps/desktop lint) after Typecheck, before Build — gates on eslint errors (default exit), NOT --max-warnings (the #282 warn-level rules stay non-blocking). New packages/framework/__tests__/lint-ci-guard.test.mjs (mirrors release-readiness-guard): asserts pr.yml contains the lint command + does NOT pass --max-warnings. framework suite 164->166. lint exit 0 (0 errors, 31 deliberate warnings). testResults: { framework: "node", command: "pnpm -C packages/framework test", passed: 166, failed: 0, skipped: 0, status: "passed" }.
